page_title: Legend of the Snow Titan: The Mystery where Nobody Gets Killed or Anything Like That
text:
is the 2nd chapter of the 7th volume and the 48th chapter overall of the Attack on Titan: Junior High manga, written and illustrated by .

Overview

 After losing their way, the first year members of the Survey Club get stranded within a hut just as a snowstorm appears. After both Jean and Reiner get knocked unconscious by various means, rumors begin to swirl of a mysterious 'Snow Titan' inhabiting the area. Is the threat coming from a mysterious new Titan, or is one of their own number the true culprit? 

Summary

During an overnight camp retreat with the Survey Club, the first year members get separated from the upperclassmen when Eren insists they follow a bearded Titan and are forced to take refuge inside a hut. When a snowstorm appears around them, Armin panics over the possibility of them running out of food; Eren notices him writing a farewell letter to his grandfather and tells him to not worry. Jean agrees that he is overreacting and places blame on Eren for getting them all separated. Connie wonders aloud if there are snow Titans within the area and recounts a tale of another group that got lost in the mountains and taken as slaves. Eren, Reiner and Bertolt begin to worry but Jean calls it ridiculous. Just then, Krista and Ymir inform the others that dinner is ready.

The next morning as they wake up, Jean is found unconscious by Krista and Ymir in the pantry. Connie points out some tracks nearby that are not human and immediately Bertolt, Eren and Armin begin panicking; Reiner reveals that his bear slippers were what made the tracks due to the cold conditions. As Bertolt and Eren question Reiner why he wears them, Armin tells himself that Jean must have merely passed out while sleepwalking the night before.

Later on, Reiner collapses while eating some food. Krista attends to him and pulls out a long string, wondering if the food in the pantry had been tampered with; Jean's presence possibly being that he stumbled across the suspect in the act and was knocked unconscious. Mikasa points out that they have no other food and must use it; when Krista tries to object, Mikasa glares at her and Krista eventually backs down.

After more suspicious activity is discovered, Eren decides to catch the snow Titan; Jean arrives and comments how stupid Eren is sounding as there are no snow Titans. He brushes his attack off as nothing but falling asleep after slipping on something in the pantry and goes of to his room. This causes Reiner to voice his suspicions of Jean himself as being the culprit; Eren doesn't buy it until a sound is heard from the back room. They discover Jean hogging a bottle of ketchup and admits to his thievery; Eren, Connie and Sasha begin beating him while Armin begins thinking it over.

The next day, Armin gathers them in the pantry to reveal that he went over everything in his head and knows the culprit is indeed one of them. To everyone's shock, Armin points an accusatory finger at Mikasa being the culprit.
